records.ety: [It. cammeo; akin to F. came, camaeu, Sp. camafeo, LL. camaeus, camahutus; of unknown origin.]

defs: A carving in relief, esp. one on a small scale used as a jewel for personal adornment, or like.
notes: ☞ Most cameos are carved in a material which has layers of different colors, such stones as the onyx and sardonyx, and various kinds of shells, being used. The classical cameos made in Italy are carved on a seashell (see cameo conch, below), having an olive figure carved from the inner layer of the shell in relief on the white background of the outer layer of the shell.
